title.en: FUNCTIONAL FEATURES OR DETAILS COMMON TO BOTH SMALLARMS AND ORDNANCE, e.g. CANNONS; MOUNTINGS FOR SMALLARMS OR ORDNANCE

Missile propulsion characterised by the use of explosive or combustible propellant charges (projecting missiles without use of explosive or combustible propellant charge F41B; launching rockets or torpedoes F41F3/00; missile self-propulsion F42B15/00)

Hypervelocity missile propulsion using successive means for increasing the propulsive force, e.g. using successively initiated propellant charges arranged along the barrel length; Multistage missile propulsion
Missile propulsion using the combustion of a liquid, {loose powder} or gaseous fuel, e.g. hypergolic fuel

Adjusting the range without varying elevation angle or propellant charge data, e.g. by venting a part of the propulsive charge gases, or by adjusting the capacity of the cartridge or combustion chamber {(adjusting the range by using gas-relieving ports in the barrel F41A21/28)}
Recoilless guns, i.e. guns having propulsion means producing no recoil
Breech mechanisms, e.g. locks
Block action, i.e. the main breech opening movement being transverse to the barrel axis
Bolt action, i.e. the main breech opening movement being parallel to the barrel axis
Breakdown breech mechanisms, e.g. for shotguns
Breech mechanisms for guns having two or more barrels (F41A3/58 takes precedence; for revolving-cannon guns F41F1/10)
using combustion gas pressure for adding to the mechanical locking action, or for delaying breech opening movement
Mounting of breech-blocks; Accessories for breech-blocks or breech-block mountings
Mechanisms or systems operated by propellant charge energy for automatically opening the lock
recoil-operated
gas-operated
Gas- or recoil-operated, e.g. selection of gas- or recoil-operated systems
Energy accumulator systems, i.e. systems for opening the breech-block by energy accumulated during barrel or gas piston recoil
Auxiliary mechanisms for bringing the breech-block or bolt or the barrel to the starting position before automatic firing; Drives for externally-powered guns; Remote-controlled gun chargers
Machine gun rechargers, e.g. manually operated (F41A3/72 takes precedence)
Drives for externally-powered guns, i.e. drives for moving the breech-block or bolt by an external force during automatic firing

Feeding or loading of ammunition ({conveying ammunition through pipes by the action of flowing gases B65G51/025} ; adaptations for feeding or loading missiles from magazines in air guns F41B11/50); Magazines; Guiding means for the extracting of cartridges (cartridge extractors or ejectors F41A15/00)
Feeding of unbelted ammunition
Feeding of belted ammunition
Feeding multibarrel guns
Feeding two or more kinds of ammunition to the same gun; Feeding from two sides
Loading arrangements, i.e. for bringing the ammunition into the firing position
Internally-powered drives, i.e. operated by propellant charge energy, e.g. couplings, clutches, energy accumulators
External power or control systems
Arrangements for changing from automatic or magazine-loading to hand-loading
Charged-condition indicators, i.e. indicating the presence of a cartridge in the cartridge chamber
Cartridge guides, stops or positioners, e.g. for cartridge extraction
Ejectors for clips or magazines, e.g. when empty
Empty-cartridge-case or belt-link collectors or catchers (F41A9/81 takes precedence)
Magazines
Reloading {or unloading} of magazines
Ammunition handling dollies or transfer carts (F41A9/86 takes precedence)
Assembly or disassembly features; Modular concepts; Articulated or collapsible guns (F41A3/64, F41A19/10 - F41A19/15, F41A21/48, F41A25/26 take precedence)
Modular concepts, e.g. weapon-family concepts
Articulated or collapsible guns, i.e. with hinged or telescopic parts for transport or storage (breakdown shotguns or rifles F41C7/11; folding or telescopic stocks or stock parts F41C23/04)
Cooling or heating systems; Blowing-through of gun barrels; Ventilating systems
Heating systems
Injecting fluids into barrels or cartridge chambers (F41A13/08 takes precedence)
Evacuating combustion gas from barrels (F41A13/10 takes precedence)
Blowers or turbines for evacuating or cooling guns, e.g. driven by combustion gas pressure or recoil
Systems for cooling the outer surface of the barrel (F41A13/10, F41A21/24 take precedence)

Cartridge extractors, i.e. devices for pulling cartridges or cartridge cases at least partially out of the cartridge chamber; Cartridge ejectors, i.e. devices for throwing the extracted cartridges or cartridge cases free of the gun (F41A9/54, {F41C9/08} take precedence; {Means for removing duds or misfires in rocket throwers F41F3/058})
for revolver-type guns, e.g. revolvers
specially adapted for cartridge cases being deformed when fired, e.g. of plastics
for breakdown guns
for block-action guns
for bolt-action guns
for guns with forwardly slidable barrels
specially adapted for caseless-ammunition duds
Tools for extracting cartridges
Safety arrangements, e.g. safeties
Key-operated safeties {(F41A17/44 takes precedence)}
Safeties of the combination-lock type (F41A17/02 takes precedence)
Electric or electromechanical safeties (F41A17/04, F41A17/08 take precedence)
for inhibiting firing in a specified direction, e.g. at a friendly person or at a protected area (F41A27/02 takes precedence)
Double-loading prevention
Cook-off prevention, i.e. prevention of spontaneous firing of a cartridge by chamber wall heat
Hang-fire prevention
Grip or stock safeties, i.e. safeties disengaged by clasping the grip or stock (thumb-operated sliding safeties F41A17/52, F41A17/62, F41A17/70, F41A17/80)
Multiple safeties, i.e. {one safety element} acting on at least one element of the firing mechanism and at least one other element of the gun, e.g. the moving barrel
Magazine safeties
Last-round safeties (F41A17/34 takes precedence)
Safeties for locking the breech-block or bolt in a safety position (F41A17/32, F41A17/36, F41A17/40 take precedence {; anti-rebound arrangements F41A3/70})
Safety plugs, e.g. for plugging-up cartridge chambers {, barrels, magazine spaces}
Trigger safeties, i.e. means for preventing trigger movement (F41A17/02 - F41A17/40 take precedence)
Sear safeties, i.e. means for rendering ineffective an intermediate lever transmitting trigger movement to firing pin, hammer, bolt or sear (F41A17/02 - F41A17/40 take precedence)
Firing-pin safeties, i.e. means for preventing movement of slidably- mounted strikers (F41A17/02 - F41A17/40 take precedence)
Hammer safeties, i.e. means for preventing the hammer from hitting the cartridge or the firing pin (F41A17/02 - F41A17/40 take precedence)
Firing or trigger mechanisms; Cocking mechanisms
Counting means indicating the number of shots fired
Shot-velocity control (F41A3/78, F41A5/28, F41A19/05, F41A19/66 take precedence)
Synchronising for firing through the propeller of an aircraft
Mechanical firing mechanisms, {e.g. counterrecoil firing, recoil actuated firing mechanisms}(F41A19/01 - F41A19/05, F41A19/59 take precedence)
Fluid-operated firing mechanisms
Firing mechanisms operating with primer cartridge
Electric firing mechanisms (F41A17/10, F41A17/12 take precedence)
Barrels; Gun tubes; Muzzle attachments; Barrel mounting means (F41A25/00 takes precedence; barrel attachments for firing grenades or riot-control ammunition from smallarms F41C27/06)
Composite barrels, i.e. barrels having multiple layers, e.g. of different materials
Plural barrels
Insert barrels, i.e. barrels for firing reduced calibre ammunition and being mounted within the normal barrels
Cartridge chambers; Chamber liners (F41A3/74, F41A9/46, F41A21/04 take precedence)
Barrels or gun tubes characterised by the shape of the bore
Barrels or gun tubes characterised by the material (F41A21/02 takes precedence)
Barrels which have undergone surface treatment, e.g. phosphating {(F41A21/44 takes precedence)}
Barrels or gun tubes with fins or ribs, e.g. for cooling (F41G1/42 takes precedence)
specially adapted for recoil reinforcement, e.g. for training purposes
Gas-expansion chambers; Barrels provided with gas-relieving ports (F41A1/06, F41A13/08 {and F41A21/36} take precedence)
Silencers
Muzzle attachments or glands (F41A21/26, F41A21/30, F41A21/46 take precedence {; for projectile velocity measurements G01P3/665, G01P3/685})
Insulation jackets; Protective jackets
Barrels having means for separating sabots from projectiles
Barrel mounting means, e.g. releasable mountings for replaceable barrels
Gun mountings, e.g. on vehicles; Disposition of guns on vehicles (F41A25/00, F41A27/00 take precedence)
{Locks for connecting guns to their mountings (F41A23/50 takes precedence)}
Mountings without wheels
for disappearing guns
Turret gun mountings (feeding, loading or guiding ammunition F41A9/00; mechanical elevating or traversing systems for turret guns F41A27/18)
Mountings for transport only; Loading or unloading arrangements for guns for use with carrier vehicles (F41A23/50 takes precedence)
Wheeled-gun mountings; Endless-track gun mountings
on wheeled or endless-track vehicles
on sledges
Trail spades
Travelling locks; Brakes for holding the gun platform in a fixed position during transport
Base plates for gun mountings

Arrangements for adjusting the gun platform in the vertical or horizontal position (F41A17/10, F41A17/12 take precedence {; ground engaging vehicle fittings for lifting or supporting it B60S9/04; supports for mobile cranes B66C23/78})
Gun mountings permitting recoil or return to battery, e.g. gun cradles; Barrel buffers or brakes (recoilless guns F41A1/08)
Fluid-operated systems
Friction-operated systems
Spring-operated systems
Hybrid systems
Bearing arrangements for the reciprocating gun-mount or barrel movement
Assembling or dismounting recoil elements or systems
Gun mountings permitting traversing or elevating movement, e.g. gun carriages
Control systems for preventing interference between the moving gun and the adjacent structure
Scatter-fire arrangements, i.e. means for oscillating guns automatically during firing
Mechanical systems (F41A27/02, F41A27/04, F41A27/30 take precedence)
Fluid-operated systems (F41A27/02, F41A27/04, F41A27/30 take precedence)
Electrically-operated systems (F41A27/02, F41A27/04, F41A27/30 take precedence)
Stabilisation or compensation systems, e.g. compensating for barrel weight or wind force {on the barrel}
Cleaning or lubricating arrangements (injecting fluids into barrels or cartridge chambers F41A13/04  {; ammunition for cleaning purposes F42B5/24})
Scrapers or cleaning rods
Lubricating, oiling or greasing means, e.g. operating during use
Testing arrangements (testing mounts F41A23/16)
for checking gun barrels

Adaptations for training (adaptations of barrels for recoil reinforcement F41A21/26); Gun simulators (teaching or practice apparatus for gun-aiming or gun-laying F41G3/26  {; shooting games A63F9/02; military simulation G09B9/003})
Light- or radiation-emitting guns {; Light- or radiation-sensitive guns; Cartridges carrying light emitting sources, e.g. laser}
Acoustical simulation of gun fire, e.g. by pyrotechnic means
Recoil simulators
Accessories or details not otherwise provided for
Dust- or weather-protection caps or covers (protecting-caps for trigger guards F41A17/54)
Adaptation of guns to both right and left hand use
Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass
